intent.use: Main overworld background bed: daytime exploring of the home valley (village, meadows, cherry orchards). The default track the player hears most, so it must be beautiful, calm, and never fatiguing.
intent.narrative: Nangijala is the land the Lionheart brothers wake up in — an afterlife valley of cherry blossoms and campfires. The music says: you have arrived somewhere kind, but it remembers the crossing; a thread of melancholy runs under the warmth.

sfx_pitching.note: Pitch one-shot SFX (footsteps, UI, chimes) to musical.key.sfx_safe_pitch_classes so they sit inside the harmony; quantize their trigger time to timing.beats_s (or downbeats_s for big moments).

sections.name: Intro - The Whispering Blossoms
sections.description: Solo wooden flute states the main melody over a soft string pad and sparse harp. The player's quietest moment — arrival, morning light.
sync_hints: Safe entry point for scene fade-in; ambience (birds, wind) should sit above the sparse mix here.
Align slow environment events (petal falls, drifting smoke) to downbeats_s.

sections.name: A-Section - The Valley Blooms
sections.description: The theme blooms: warm legato strings carry the melody, harp keeps flowing eighth-note arpeggios, frame-drum heartbeat becomes audible.
sync_hints: Good moment for the world to feel alive: NPC activity, animals, waterwheel motion synced to beats_s.
Footstep SFX pitched to sfx_safe_notes read as part of the arrangement here.

sections.name: B-Section - Over the Green Meadows
sections.description: Emotional high point: French horn countermelody and soft choir-like pad widen the sound. Vista moment.
sync_hints: Best section for reveals (vista opens, title card, quest banner) — trigger on this section's first downbeat.
Use events.peaks inside this window for one-shot sparkle/light effects.

sections.name: Outro - Campfire Embers Fade
sections.description: The theme returns, gentler — strings soften, harp thins out. Warmth with the melancholy thread most audible.
sync_hints: Good window for quiet dialogue or journal UI — mix leaves headroom for voice/UI sounds.

events.note: onsets_s = every audible attack (fire discrete FX here); peaks = the strongest hits (thunder/flash-worthy).

dynamics.note: 50 ms loudness curve in dBFS — drive continuous effects (light, fog, camera) from this, index = t / hop_s.

loop.note: Generated audio is not sample-loop-perfect; crossfade loop_end into loop_start over crossfade_ms.

engine.prompt: A warm, mythical orchestral fantasy theme for the overworld of a storybook adventure game, set in a peaceful afterlife valley of cherry blossoms, green meadows and campfire smoke. Cinematic but intimate, in the spirit of Howard Shore's Shire music and gentle Nordic folk. Structure: a solo wooden flute opens alone with a simple, singable melody over a soft string pad and sparse fingerpicked harp; the full theme then blooms in warm legato strings with the harp keeping flowing eighth-note arpeggios; a wide-eyed middle section lifts with a distant French horn countermelody and a soft wordless-choir-like pad, the emotional high point; then the theme settles back down to the quiet solo flute and strings for a gentle, unresolved, loop-friendly ending that can flow back into the beginning. A faint, soft frame-drum heartbeat keeps a clear gentle pulse throughout. Strictly instrumental. D major, 76 BPM, 4/4. High-fidelity, lush, spacious cinematic mix with soft dynamics and a touch of wistful melancholy under the warmth. Avoid: vocals, sung lyrics, singing, spoken word, chiptune, 8-bit, lo-fi, distortion, harsh percussion, EDM, trap beat, abrupt ending, fade to silence mid-phrase.
